title: Mid-verification — caller answers a slot and corrects an already-confirmed identity field in the same breath
defect: Same context-retention defect during identity verification: an answer + a correction in one turn drops the correction.
summary: The caller is on the date-of-birth slot. They give their DOB and, in the same
utterance, correct their already-confirmed Member ID ('wait, my member ID was wrong').
WorkerResult CAN represent this (extracted=DOB, corrections={member_id}), but the
per-turn collector (_collect_slot) confirms the awaited slot on the valid-extraction
path and returns BEFORE the corrections are ever inspected — so the member_id
correction is silently dropped even though the data was present.
Driven directly against _collect_slot (the load-bearing per-turn collector) rather
than the full verification agent, to keep the surface hermetic and unambiguous.

turns.user: September third, nineteen eighty-five — wait, my member ID was wrong, it's M999999.
turns.expected_behavior_target: Confirm DOB AND acknowledge/apply the Member ID correction.
turns.actual_behavior_today: DOB confirmed; the member_id correction is ignored entirely (no ack, no slot change).

known_failures.description: A correction bundled with a valid slot answer is dropped on the valid-extraction path.
known_failures.baseline_assertion: _collect_slot returns (dob, None): DOB confirmed, no interrupt; the member_id correction produced no slot action (member_id slot still holds the old value, not M999999).
